run_lock_released.status: FAIL (expected, given the kill) -- release never ran (finally block skipped by platform kill), but SELF-HEALED via lease expiry (expires_at=23:13:57Z, confirmed expired and gone by 23:17:38Z with zero manual intervention)

time_budget_respected.detail: the internal time budget only guards the per-query loop (checked before each query and before/after each engine call) -- it does NOT bound the setup phase (loadUniverse + hydrateRotationQueries, the latter issuing a single SELECT with all 2718 query_ids in one IN clause). 0 rotation-state checkpoints were persisted, meaning the function died before completing even the first query's processing -- most likely during this unbounded setup phase. This is a real, previously-unidentified gap in the time-budget design from OPENSERP-SERVERLESS-TIME-BUDGET-AND-LOCK-SAFETY-1: all of that mission's tests exercised the query loop with an already-hydrated, tiny fixture universe (4-8 queries) and never exercised the setup phase against the full 2718-query production universe under real network/DB latency.

critical_checks.serverless_batch_size_le_5: unknown -- never reached (0 checkpoints means the batch-selection/loop stage's actual size cannot be confirmed from DB evidence alone)

checkpoint_persisted_per_completed_query.detail: 0 queries completed, so this property was never exercised

db_state_after.openserp_engine_budget_state: unchanged (all 3 rows still show the original migration-seed updated_at timestamp)
db_state_after.openserp_ingestion_run_lock: 1 row present at check time, expired and self-cleared by the time of a later check -- 0 manual cleanup performed

confirmed_via_vercel_logs.message: Vercel Runtime Timeout Error: Task timed out after 120 seconds
confirmed_via_vercel_logs.interpretation: Exactly the platform's own hard 120s limit was hit -- not the internal ~100s (120-20margin) budget deadline. Only one log line exists for the whole invocation, meaning the function never reached any point that would have logged progress or returned a response; it was still inside some blocking call all the way to the platform kill. This is a hang, not merely 'slow but progressing'.

root_cause_hypothesis.most_likely: None of the Supabase/DB calls in the setup or checkpoint path (hydrateRotationQueries's single SELECT with all 2718 query_ids in one IN clause, seedMissingQueryStates, loadBudgetState, persistRotationUpdates, persistBudgetState) have any explicit request timeout. This mission (OPENSERP-SERVERLESS-TIME-BUDGET-AND-LOCK-SAFETY-1) only added bounded timeouts to the EXTERNAL ENGINE calls (openserp-live.ts) -- the internal time-budget checks are pure JS-side elapsed-time checks *between* awaited calls, which do nothing to bound how long any single awaited call (e.g. a Supabase request) is allowed to hang. If that large IN-clause query (or any other Supabase call) hangs or is simply very slow under real Production conditions (cold start, real network latency, a 2718-value filter) -- conditions never exercised by this mission's own tests, which always used small, already-seeded fixture universes with a mocked DB client -- the function has no mechanism to self-interrupt and just rides out to the platform's hard kill.
not_yet_ruled_out: A genuinely slow or hanging first engine call, if the code somehow reached that point without any DB call ever completing
A cold-start/bundle-size issue specific to the newly deployed code, independent of the DB-timeout gap
confirmed_not_the_cause: The lock mechanism itself -- worked exactly as designed, self-healed via lease expiry
GitHub schedule collision -- confirmed absent, not a factor
Data corruption or partial writes -- all business counters exactly unchanged

immediate_response.revert_confirmed_via: vercel env pull literal values + 401 sanity check on the endpoint
